feature: Support enforcement of Possession Orders after case archival
hlFunction: Allow claimants to initiate enforcement of a Possession Order even after the associated case has been archived or deleted due to data retention rules, by providing a process to validate and act on the enforcement request without requiring full case data to be present.
eventTrigger:
A claimant receives a Possession Order.
The Possession Order is not enforced for 6 years.
The case is archived or deleted in accordance with retention rules.
Then, the claimant initiates enforcement of the Possession Order.
userStory: to be able to enforce a Possession Order even after the case has been archived or deleted due to retention rules so that I can exercise my legal right to enforcement even if I didn’t act within the original retention period.
expectedOutcomes:
Given a Possession Order was issued and not enforced within 6 years,
And the case has been archived or deleted due to data retention policies,
When the claimant decides to enforce the Possession Order,
Then there must be a way for them to initiate enforcement even though the original case record is no longer active.
notes: Requirement requested by Lucy and verifed by Flora/Rhona
